title: The One With Chandler And Monica's Weddin

jokes.text: From now on, it's you four and me and the missus. The little woman. The wife. The old ball and chain.
jokes.setup: Chandler referring to Monica in traditional married man terms
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of increasingly dated/sexist terms for wife
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Chandler's neurotic relationship with marriage through escalating clichés

jokes.text: Old? - The young, hot ball and chain.
jokes.setup: Monica objects to being called 'old'
jokes.punchline: Chandler keeps the sexist term but adds flattering adjectives
jokes.explanation: Quick, economical fix to the previous joke with good character interplay

jokes.text: Joey, you too? I just heard 'lunch.' Yeah, I can go. Sure.
jokes.setup: Joey being invited somewhere with the women
jokes.punchline: He only heard the word 'lunch' and that's enough for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey characterization - his food obsession overrides everything else. Economical and true to character.

jokes.text: If you need money, it's a bad time. I'm buying 128 dinners tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Ross wants to talk about something that might be awkward
jokes.punchline: Chandler assumes it's about money and cites the specific wedding expense
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor about wedding costs, specific number makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'm not gonna say this as your friend... but as Monica's older brother. - But you're still my friend? - Not for a few minutes. - Right now, are you still my best man? - No.
jokes.setup: Ross switching roles for a serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Chandler's increasingly specific questions about their relationship status
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of an absurd premise with good character work from both actors

jokes.text: Do I still call you Ross?
jokes.setup: Continuation of the role-switching confusion
jokes.punchline: Chandler taking the logic to its extreme conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the absurd role-switching bit, economical and character-driven

jokes.text: What? I'm serious.
jokes.setup: Ross has just given an over-the-top protective brother speech
jokes.punchline: His confusion at Chandler's reaction suggests he was genuinely threatening
jokes.explanation: Ross's obliviousness to how intense he sounded creates the comedy

jokes.text: You won't believe what Monica's brother just said to me!
jokes.setup: The audience just saw the entire threatening conversation
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately running to tell people about it
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Chandler's reaction and setting up future complications

jokes.text: I'm responsible for all those. I had to go with the odds.
jokes.setup: Monica lists wedding disasters she's worried about
jokes.punchline: Phoebe admits she's responsible for the most likely problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - acknowledging her own flakiness with mathematical certainty

jokes.text: Back then we called it 'The Great War.' It really was.
jokes.setup: Discussion of WWI period movie
jokes.punchline: Jack speaks as if he lived through WWI, then doubles down
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Jack's tendency to overidentify with historical events

jokes.text: See you. I'm off to fight the Nazis.
jokes.setup: Joey excited about his WWI movie role
jokes.punchline: He's confused about which war had which enemies
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey confusion, perfectly in character and sets up the correction

jokes.text: Wait, Joey. We fought the Nazis in WWII, not WWI. Who was in WWI?
jokes.setup: Ross correcting Joey's historical confusion
jokes.punchline: But then Ross realizes he doesn't know the answer either
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - Ross starts to be the smart one but reveals his own ignorance

jokes.text: Who did we fight in WWI? - Mexico? - Yes. Very good.
jokes.setup: Joey needing to know WWI enemies for his role
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong answer that Ross affirms rather than admit ignorance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of ignorance with Ross choosing to enable rather than educate

jokes.text: I can't believe this. This is incredible. You just won an Oscar. - No, I didn't. - I think you did. - I think I lost, three times.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to impress a famous actor
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction that gets worse with each exchange
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy escalation with Joey's enthusiasm meeting harsh reality

jokes.text: I thought it might be a cool character thing. He's a face toucher.
jokes.setup: Joey being caught doing something distracting during the scene
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous justification and character choice description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey trying to sound professional while being completely unprofessional

jokes.text: Can my dad borrow one of your pearl necklaces? Did he say what kind of neckline he's wearing?
jokes.setup: Monica asking to borrow jewelry for her dad
jokes.punchline: Rachel treating it as a normal fashion consultation
jokes.explanation: Rachel's practical response to an absurd situation creates the humor

jokes.text: I just met him once, but I'm guessing... plunging? He is more of a if-you've-got-it-flaunt-it kind of father.
jokes.setup: Continuing the neckline discussion about Chandler's cross-dressing father
jokes.punchline: Rachel's assessment of his fashion sense and Monica's agreement
jokes.explanation: Good continuation building on the absurdity with character-specific details

jokes.text: Yes, include more people in this.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting they ask more people about the jewelry situation
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response to expanding the embarrassing request
jokes.explanation: Monica's control-freak nature showing through sarcasm about wedding complications

jokes.text: I have a date tomorrow night.
jokes.setup: Monica talking about their wedding being tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Chandler referring to their wedding as a 'date'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler understatement humor - massive understatement delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Well, Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica about to have a characteristic freakout about wedding planning
jokes.punchline: She catches herself mid-freakout and stops
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Monica starting to spiral but catching herself - character growth moment

jokes.text: If you're calling after Saturday, you've reached Mr. And Mrs. Bing. Please leave a message for... the Bings!
jokes.setup: Recording their post-wedding answering machine message
jokes.punchline: Their over-enthusiasm about being 'the Bings'
jokes.explanation: Sweet but slightly cringey enthusiasm that's very true to their relationship

jokes.text: We're the Bings.
jokes.setup: Meeting Monica's cousin at the rehearsal dinner
jokes.punchline: They immediately test out their new identity
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing their continued enthusiasm about their new married identity

jokes.text: Thanks for wearing... something.
jokes.setup: Chandler greeting his cross-dressing father at the rehearsal dinner
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his father wearing clothes at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm dealing with his father's unconventional appearance

jokes.text: This is so exciting! I thought we screwed you up too much for this.
jokes.setup: Mother expressing excitement about her son's wedding
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that she thought they psychologically damaged him
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny admission of parental failure delivered with cheerful obliviousness

jokes.text: Just think, soon there will be lots of little Bings.
jokes.setup: Mother enthusiastically talking about future grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Given the family's issues, 'little Bings' sounds ominous
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in the idea of perpetuating the Bing family dysfunction

jokes.text: I can't believe I'm going back to work to get spit on again.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about having to return to work
jokes.punchline: The specific indignity he's returning to
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag about method acting and spitting

jokes.text: You're lucky. At least the people that spit on you are famous.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about being spit on
jokes.punchline: Ross finding the positive angle in an objectively bad situation
jokes.explanation: Good Ross logic - finding academic perspective on Joey's gross situation

jokes.text: Are you his mother or father?
jokes.setup: Jack meeting Chandler's cross-dressing father
jokes.punchline: Innocent but inappropriate question about gender identity
jokes.explanation: Jack's oblivious directness creating maximum awkwardness

jokes.text: What? I've never seen one before.
jokes.setup: Monica objecting to her father's inappropriate question
jokes.punchline: Jack's defense makes it worse by treating it like a museum exhibit
jokes.explanation: Jack's innocent explanation somehow makes the situation more uncomfortable

jokes.text: I wasn't even able to act like I'm okay with it.
jokes.setup: Monica dealing with her father's behavior
jokes.punchline: Her admission that she can't even pretend to handle this situation
jokes.explanation: Monica's control issues extending to not being able to fake composure

jokes.text: Aren't you a little old to wear a dress like that? Don't you have a little too much penis to wear a dress like that?
jokes.setup: Judy making an ageist comment about Charles's dress
jokes.punchline: Charles's devastating comeback about gender appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comeback that turns the tables completely while being perfectly phrased

jokes.text: Of course you can kick his ass, son. You could kick anyone's ass you want to.
jokes.setup: Ross still upset about the earlier confrontation
jokes.punchline: Jack's unconditional but completely unrealistic support
jokes.explanation: Perfect father support that's both sweet and completely delusional about Ross's abilities

jokes.text: He's the man in the black dress.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking how to identify Chandler's dad
jokes.punchline: The perfectly practical but absurd description
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical description that treats the absurd situation matter-of-factly

jokes.text: A man, duh!
jokes.setup: Charles introduces himself as Amanda
jokes.punchline: Rachel thinks it's a clever pun rather than his actual name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Rachel's misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: Dr. Ross Geller. Dad, please. As I was saying... I'm Dr. Ross Geller.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to start his best man speech
jokes.punchline: Jack's interruption and Ross's immediate repetition of the title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross ego moment - can't resist emphasizing his doctorate even when asked not to

jokes.text: And he would've married her, only she couldn't control her bladder. It was his dog. Did I say it was his dog?
jokes.setup: Ross telling embarrassing story about Chandler's relationship
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was about a dog, then awkwardly tries to clarify
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke that gets funnier with Ross's awkward backtracking

jokes.text: Maybe I can wear the dress for Halloween!
jokes.setup: Monica sad that the wedding will be over
jokes.punchline: Her practical solution to extend the dress's usefulness
jokes.explanation: Sweet Monica logic - finding practical uses for everything, even her wedding dress

jokes.text: I said, I'll do it. Who wants it? Anybody? I said, I'll do it. Nobody? All right, I'll do it myself. I'm not gonna screw it up.
jokes.setup: Monica asking who will steam her dress safely
jokes.punchline: Monica completely ignoring Phoebe's repeated offers to help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica control freak moment with great escalation as she ignores obvious help

jokes.text: What grandmother?
jokes.setup: Monica thanking Phoebe for helping her grandmother
jokes.punchline: Phoebe revealing she has no idea what Monica is talking about
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that Phoebe wasn't actually responsible for this task

jokes.text: And, if I remember correctly, Ray-Ban was the official sponsor of WWI.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why he's wearing sunglasses to avoid spit
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up historical fact to justify the anachronism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic combining his historical ignorance with product placement awareness

jokes.text: Maybe it's not what we think. Maybe it's: 'Tell Monica I'm sorry I drank the last of the milk.' Or 'Tell Monica I'm sorry I used her tweezers to pluck my nose hair.' He does that. I've seen him.
jokes.setup: Finding Chandler's 'sorry' note and assuming he left
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's increasingly mundane alternative explanations with TMI details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect Phoebe oversharing about disgusting details

jokes.text: Or maybe he was writing to tell her that he's changed his name: 'Tell Monica I'm Sorry.'
jokes.setup: Continuing alternative explanations for the note
jokes.punchline: Treating 'Sorry' as a proper name rather than an apology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's both absurd and grammatically logical, peak Phoebe creativity

jokes.text: Good God, isn't it possible that 'Sorry' is sitting in there right now?
jokes.setup: Ross dismissing her name-change theory
jokes.punchline: Phoebe doubling down on treating 'Sorry' as a person
jokes.explanation: Great escalation as Phoebe commits fully to her absurd theory

jokes.text: But if you don't find him and bring him back... I am gonna hunt you down and kick your ass!
jokes.setup: Ross promising to find Chandler
jokes.punchline: Rachel using Ross's exact threatening language from earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing how Ross's dramatic language is spreading to others

jokes.text: I just hope that someday you guys find someone like Chandler. I hope we find someone exactly like Chandler and soon.
jokes.setup: Monica gushing about how wonderful Chandler is
jokes.punchline: Rachel and Phoebe's desperate agreement while knowing he's missing
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as the audience knows what Monica doesn't

jokes.text: Could you lower the script? I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Joey asking his scene partner to lower his script
jokes.punchline: Richard's blunt refusal with no explanation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Joey's reasonable request and Richard's diva behavior

jokes.text: That's what real actors do. Enunciation is the mark of a good actor. And when you enunciate, you spit!
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about being spit on during scenes
jokes.punchline: Richard's passionate defense of spitting as good acting technique
jokes.explanation: Great method actor parody with Richard treating spitting as high art

jokes.text: Then we'll see you the day after tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Gunther saying he wasn't invited to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Ross's polite but awkward response to the social misstep
jokes.explanation: Ross trying to smooth over the awkwardness with overly specific timing

jokes.text: Gunther? She just added him to her list.
jokes.setup: Judy commenting on the 'sexy blond' behind the counter
jokes.punchline: Reference to the friends' 'list' of celebrities they can sleep with
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier series mythology with Jack's misunderstanding

jokes.text: And if you see Rita Moreno, let her know I'm looking for her.
jokes.setup: Ross asking his parents to watch for Chandler
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of his celebrity crush from his 'list'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's priorities even in crisis

jokes.text: Great scene! You were awesome. In that last speech, you soaked me.
jokes.setup: Joey and Richard finishing their dramatic scene
jokes.punchline: Joey treating excessive spitting as a compliment on the performance
jokes.explanation: Joey's growth as he starts to accept the method acting approach

jokes.text: No, you gotta get me out of it. I have plans. Important plans!
jokes.setup: Joey learning he has to work the next day
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate pleas about his 'important plans'
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's dedication to his friends over his career advancement

jokes.text: I'm getting married today! I cracked a rib, but I don't care. Today's my wedding! My day is finally here!
jokes.setup: Monica waking up on her wedding day
jokes.punchline: Her manic enthusiasm overriding physical pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica intensity - nothing will dampen her wedding day enthusiasm

jokes.text: She might not even notice he's gone.
jokes.setup: Monica's manic wedding day enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's deadpan assessment that Monica is oblivious
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Monica's obliviousness and the reality of the situation

jokes.text: But she'll be in the gown and then he won't show up... then she'll have to take off the gown... Stop it. You can't do this here.
jokes.setup: Rachel imagining the worst-case scenario
jokes.punchline: Her spiraling into catastrophic thinking until Phoebe stops her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's tendency to catastrophize

jokes.text: This is Monica's bathroom, right?
jokes.setup: Rachel and Phoebe looking for tissues
jokes.punchline: Rachel's question sets up the pregnancy test discovery
jokes.explanation: Important setup for the major plot revelation, gains significance on rewatch

jokes.text: There was a pregnancy test in the garbage. And it's positive. Monica's pregnant.
jokes.setup: Looking for tissues in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Major plot revelation that changes everything
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist delivered perfectly - changes the entire dynamic of the episode

jokes.text: So I guess she won't be totally alone.
jokes.setup: Rachel's catastrophic vision of Monica alone after being left at the altar
jokes.punchline: Phoebe finding the silver lining in the pregnancy news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - finding the positive in a potentially devastating situation

jokes.text: Can this count as her 'something new'?
jokes.setup: Learning about Monica's pregnancy on her wedding day
jokes.punchline: Treating the pregnancy as fulfilling wedding tradition
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between pregnancy and wedding traditions, very Phoebe perspective

jokes.text: It's turning into the worst wedding ever. The bride's pregnant, the groom's missing and I'm still holding this.
jokes.setup: All the wedding disasters piling up
jokes.punchline: Rachel's final complaint about still holding the pregnancy test
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the mundane complaint adding to the absurdity

jokes.text: Just for the future. This is hardly a commercial for it.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about Monica's birth control method
jokes.punchline: Her practical assessment of its effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic treating failed contraception as bad advertising

jokes.text: Pretty sure they both think I'm interested in them.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to call Chandler's parents without revealing he's missing
jokes.punchline: His awkward cover story backfiring in an uncomfortable way
jokes.explanation: Good Ross awkwardness as his well-meaning efforts create new problems

jokes.text: I have documented night blindness.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Ross sucks at searching
jokes.punchline: Ross's ridiculous excuse for his failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross behavior - making up medical conditions to avoid admitting failure

jokes.text: I'm never gonna get married! Maybe I should just forget about it. Become a lesbian or something.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to stall Monica
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic spiral into alternative life choices
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperate distraction attempt with characteristic drama

jokes.text: Maybe I'd feel better if I slept with Joey.
jokes.setup: Rachel continuing her fake crisis
jokes.punchline: Taking her desperation to an uncomfortable extreme
jokes.explanation: Rachel's stalling technique getting increasingly desperate and inappropriate

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm marrying my best friend. Isn't that beautiful?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to get out of work by claiming he's getting married
jokes.punchline: His romantic spin on marrying Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good Joey improvisation that accidentally sounds romantic about his friendship

jokes.text: It's not on my head.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming he needs hair transplant surgery
jokes.punchline: Implying the hair transplant is for a more private area
jokes.explanation: Economical delivery of an uncomfortable implication that's very Joey

jokes.text: I'm wearing two belts.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if Richard is drunk
jokes.punchline: Richard's absurd visual proof of his intoxication
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual gag that perfectly captures drunk logic

jokes.text: That is an old Chinese woman.
jokes.setup: Rachel thinking she spotted Chandler
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact correction of her obvious mistake
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Rachel's desperation and Ross's literal-mindedness

jokes.text: He could be in disguise, you know.
jokes.setup: Ross pointing out her mistake
jokes.punchline: Rachel doubling down on her impossible theory
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperate logic leading to increasingly absurd theories

jokes.text: How'd you guys find me? I knew I should've hid at the gym.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel finding Chandler at his office
jokes.punchline: Chandler's joke about hiding at a place he'd never go
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecating humor about his own lack of athletic activity

jokes.text: Panicking. And using the Internet to try to prove that I'm related to Monica.
jokes.setup: Ross asking what Chandler is doing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's desperate attempt to find a way out of marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chandler panic logic - using technology for completely absurd purposes

jokes.text: Have you ever put on a black dress and asked me up to your hotel room?
jokes.setup: Chandler worried about becoming like his parents
jokes.punchline: Ross's specific test to prove Chandler isn't like his father
jokes.explanation: Good Ross logic using very specific evidence to make his point

jokes.text: I don't race cars or make popcorn. None of my proceeds go to charity.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying only Paul Newman can make marriage work
jokes.punchline: His itemized list of why he's not Paul Newman
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler specificity in listing Paul Newman's exact qualifications

jokes.text: Right now no one has a lower opinion of you than I do. But I totally believe you can do this.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to encourage Chandler
jokes.punchline: His brutal honesty followed by genuine support
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ross logic - insulting someone as motivation, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Depends on what you mean by 'we.'
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting they go home and take a shower
jokes.punchline: Chandler's concern about Ross's pronoun choice
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler paranoia about Ross's overly intimate language

jokes.text: Good old Chandler with the homophobic jokes.
jokes.setup: Chandler making a joke about showering together
jokes.punchline: Ross calling out the pattern of Chandler's humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Chandler's character type while showing Ross's awareness

jokes.text: Who's Mike Smith? I was sure this was my office.
jokes.setup: Chandler in what he thought was his office
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been in the wrong place the whole time
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that adds to Chandler's overall confusion and panic

jokes.text: The days and nights are hard, I get it!
jokes.setup: Phoebe's overly philosophical rambling about time
jokes.punchline: Rachel cutting through the pretentious speech
jokes.explanation: Rachel's impatience with Phoebe's philosophical tangents

jokes.text: That's such a hard time for me.
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning the wedding is at dusk
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating dusk as personally challenging
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - making everything about her personal spiritual journey

jokes.text: Oh, good God, I've fallen down.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to stall Monica
jokes.punchline: Her desperate physical comedy to buy time
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperation leading to physical comedy as a stalling tactic

jokes.text: Found the vest. We'll have to keep an eye on it, make sure we don't lose it again.
jokes.setup: Phoebe revealing they were lying about Chandler's vest
jokes.punchline: Her continued commitment to the lie
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the vest lie with Phoebe maintaining the fiction

jokes.text: For a minute, I was like, 'Oh, my God! The worst has happened!'
jokes.setup: Monica relieved about the fake vest crisis
jokes.punchline: Her statement about 'the worst' while unaware of the real problems
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony as Monica has no idea about the real disasters

jokes.text: Look, my best friends are getting married in, like, an hour, okay? And I'm the minister. Please, can you pull it together?
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about Richard's drunk acting
jokes.punchline: Joey lecturing about professionalism while being equally unprofessional
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Joey demanding professionalism while being a walking disaster himself

jokes.text: Slippery little bugger!
jokes.setup: Richard trying to act while drunk
jokes.punchline: His comment about dropping his prop sword
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Richard's drunken inability to handle props

jokes.text: At least I think that was what he said. It was difficult to understand with his legs wrapped around my head.
jokes.setup: Charles talking about his ex-marriage
jokes.punchline: Escalating the uncomfortable revelation with graphic details
jokes.explanation: Charles's oversharing creating maximum uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: I'm so happy for me.
jokes.setup: Monica in her wedding dress receiving compliments
jokes.punchline: Her self-centered version of wedding day happiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica narcissism delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Damn it!
jokes.setup: Learning that Chandler has been found and returned
jokes.punchline: Ross's disappointment at missing more drama
jokes.explanation: Ross's weird disappointment that the crisis is resolved

jokes.text: Don't hang up on me! I'll marry you and me right now! I have the power!
jokes.setup: Joey being hung up on while trying to officiate remotely
jokes.punchline: His desperate assertion of his ministerial authority
jokes.explanation: Great Joey desperation with his power-mad moment about his ministerial role

jokes.text: Anastassakis Papasifakis wedding. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Ross looking for other weddings at the hotel
jokes.punchline: His appreciation for the extremely Greek names
jokes.explanation: Ross's academic appreciation for authentic ethnic names

jokes.text: They're my friends, Monica Stephanopolis. And Chandler Acidophilus.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to convince the Greek priest to officiate
jokes.punchline: His fake Greek names using yogurt culture terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining Greek-sounding names with his scientific knowledge

jokes.text: That was after the wedding. It's not bad luck then. Honey, it isn't good luck.
jokes.setup: Charles defending his cross-dressing at his own wedding
jokes.punchline: Nora's practical assessment that it's not fortunate either
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth between the divorced couple about their unconventional marriage

jokes.text: These are beads of joy. Don't touch me.
jokes.setup: Rachel claiming she's sweating from happiness
jokes.punchline: Her immediate contradiction with physical rejection
jokes.explanation: Rachel's stress showing through her contradictory behavior

jokes.text: Why would you play hide-and-seek with someone you know is a flight risk?
jokes.setup: Learning that Ross lost Chandler again
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's logical criticism of Ross's approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic pointing out the obvious flaw in Ross's plan

jokes.text: You wouldn't happen to have a very big fork?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to get Richard unconscious to leave work
jokes.punchline: His request for an improvised weapon
jokes.explanation: Dark Joey humor as he considers violence to solve his problem

jokes.text: Is that my ass?
jokes.setup: Richard being carried out unconscious
jokes.punchline: His confused question about seeing his own body
jokes.explanation: Absurd drunk logic as Richard questions his own body parts

jokes.text: You're not walking out on my sister! I'm serious! What, you'll 'hi-yah'?
jokes.setup: Ross confronting Chandler aggressively
jokes.punchline: Phoebe mocking Ross's martial arts threat
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Phoebe deflating Ross's tough guy act

jokes.text: And I thought, 'Anything that can fit into this can't be scary.' You obviously didn't see Chucky III.
jokes.setup: Chandler showing tiny baby shoes to explain his confidence
jokes.punchline: Ross's reference to the horror movie about a killer doll
jokes.explanation: Good setup-punchline with Ross's logical but dark counterargument

jokes.text: I've never walked down the aisle knowing it can't end in divorce.
jokes.setup: Charles walking down the aisle at his son's wedding
jokes.punchline: His comment about the permanence of gay relationships at the time
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about his relationship status with social commentary

jokes.text: Not that old crone. My mother.
jokes.setup: Wishing Chandler's grandmother could see the wedding
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she means the other grandmother
jokes.explanation: Nora's casual cruelty about the living grandmother

jokes.text: Is this new? Not now.
jokes.setup: Monica noticing Chandler's boutonniere during the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Chandler's exasperated response to her timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica obsessing over details even during her wedding ceremony

jokes.text: I can take it from here. Thanks.
jokes.setup: The Greek priest starting the ceremony
jokes.punchline: Joey asserting his authority over his own job
jokes.explanation: Joey finally arriving to do his job after all the chaos

jokes.text: You may be confused by this now... but you won't be Memorial Day weekend, 2002.
jokes.setup: Joey apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: His cryptic reference to future understanding
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke referencing the show's future episodes, very clever fourth-wall break

jokes.text: Let's get started before the groom takes off again.
jokes.setup: Joey ready to start the ceremony
jokes.punchline: His reference to Chandler's recent flight risk behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback acknowledging the day's drama while moving forward

jokes.text: And now, as I've left my notes in my dressing room... we shall proceed to the vows.
jokes.setup: Joey conducting the wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: His admission that he's unprepared for his most important moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - managing to be unprofessional even at the crucial moment

jokes.text: He took off? Go on. Go on.
jokes.setup: Joey accidentally revealing Chandler ran away
jokes.punchline: Monica's momentary shock followed by immediate refocus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica determination to push through even shocking revelations

jokes.text: Unless you don't want to.
jokes.setup: Monica finishing her romantic wedding vows
jokes.punchline: Her last-second doubt and escape clause
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Monica moment - even her wedding vows include an out clause

jokes.text: Oh, wait. Do you take each other? I do. I do. Yeah, you do!
jokes.setup: Joey realizing he skipped part of the ceremony
jokes.punchline: His awkward backtracking and enthusiastic confirmation
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey unprofessionalism saved by genuine enthusiasm

jokes.text: What baby? Our baby. We have a baby?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentioning he knows about the baby
jokes.punchline: Monica's confusion revealing it's not her pregnancy test
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection revealing the pregnancy test mystery isn't solved yet

jokes.text: Look at them. And they're gonna have a baby.
jokes.setup: Everyone confused about whose pregnancy test it was
jokes.punchline: Camera revealing Ross and Rachel together, setting up future storylines
jokes.explanation: Perfect season-ending reveal that recontextualizes everything and sets up major future plots
